GOOGLE IS BUILDING A CHIP WITH GEMINI BAKED INTO THE SILICON
articles.summary: Google is reportedly working on a project that would bake Gemini's neural-network architecture right into the circuitry of a chip. This would lock the model into the chip, increasing efficiency and decreasing overhead. The model can still be refreshed by loading new weights, but the underlying structure will stay fixed. While the project may not end up as a finished product, it shows how the custom AI silicon industry may be moving away from running any model to fusing one model with the metal.

articles.title: AMD LAUNCHES HELIOS, ITS FIRST RACK AI SYSTEM TO RIVAL NVIDIA, ADDING MICROSOFT AS NEWEST BUYER
articles.summary: Helios is a rack-scale system that combines AMD's GPUs, CPUs, networking, and software to power frontier model inference. It will start shipping to customers later this year. AMD hasn't released any details about cost, but it is estimated that Helios will cost between $5 million and $5.5 million. AMD only holds around 4.5% of the data center GPU market, but Helios could change that.

articles.title: HUMAN MATHEMATICIANS ARE BEING OUTCOUNTEREXAMPLED
articles.summary: This post looks at what's going on in the world of formalization, AI tools, and counterexamples. AI tools are now starting to solve theorems. They can even write proofs in Lean to dispel any doubts about their validity. This is speeding up the research process for mathematicians.

articles.title: BRAINCO DEMONSTRATES BRAIN-CONTROLLED ROBOT AI PLATFORM
articles.summary: BrainCo showcased a platform that allows users to direct robots through neural signals at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference this week. The technology uses an EEG headset to pick up the wearer's brain signals. AI algorithms are used to decode the signals into intent, which then gets converted into commands for the robot. The demonstration showed a mind-controlled robotic arm completing tasks that require precision, such as grasping a cup and picking up an apple.

articles.title: [WEBINAR] 8 LEVELS OF CONTEXT MATURITY IN AI-NATIVE ENGINEERING
articles.summary: AI is in your development workflow. While the token spend shows it, the throughput doesn't. The human is very much still in the loop, and that's a context problem. Join live (FREE) on Jul 23 [12] to see why teams get stuck and how leading teams use a context engine to fix it.

articles.title: AGENT SWARMS AND THE NEW MODEL ECONOMICS
articles.summary: Every jump in AI capability raises the level of abstraction at which an engineer can work. With agent swarms, the unit of work becomes the spec. For this to work, the swarm has to actually follow the spec. This post discusses how to do that.

articles.title: THE PRODUCTIVITY-EXPERIENCE PARADOX
articles.summary: AI is brilliant for external goods - money, status, and output - and potentially corrosive for internal ones - the skill, the mastery, and the satisfaction you can only get by doing the thing itself. People after external goods see AI as close to a superpower, as it makes people more productive and produces more output. However, for those interested in the internal goods, AI can feel like a loss, as it takes the parts they loved and hands it to a machine, leaving only supervision and accountability. While the output still arrives, the pride and satisfaction of owning the work is gone.

articles.title: WHO'S AFRAID OF CHINESE MODELS?
articles.summary: The industry is overreacting to Kimi K3 and Chinese open-weight models. However, there is reason to be concerned about cybersecurity risks. Models capable of mounting cybersecurity attacks on existing infrastructure are already widely available. The only viable defense is to make sure defenders have access to the best models as well.

articles.title: GOOGLE IS BUILDING AN AI FENCE AROUND THE INTERNET IT ONCE CHAMPIONED
articles.summary: Google revamped its search with AI last year. Its AI mode replaces search results with conversational responses. People are spending more time with Google than ever, but the company is sending less and less traffic to sites through search results. The changes threaten the open web and show how rapidly AI has upended the technology landscape.

articles.title: ONE DOCUMENT, TWO HANDS

articles.title: TAIWAN INDICTS EX-TSMC MANAGER FOR ALLEGEDLY STEALING CHIP SECRETS FOR CHINA
articles.summary: The former deputy manager, who faces a sentence of up to seven years, allegedly copied 21 confidential documents intending to use them in China.

articles.title: ENGINEERING MANAGEMENT AFTER THE COST OF CODE COLLAPSED
articles.summary: Headcount is now more about how much accountability you can afford rather than an indicator of capacity.

articles.summary: This post looks at factors that organizations should adopt to be smaller, faster, and deliver outsized value to customers.

articles.title: A CHINESE AI LAB JUST BUILT A GIANT DATA CENTRE WITH NO NVIDIA INSIDE
articles.summary: Z.ai has started partial operations at a giant data center that runs only on Chinese-made chips.
